name: First Federal Savings And Loan Association Of Greensburg
description_short: Community-focused savings and loan institution in Greensburg, Indiana offering personal and business banking with local decision-making and relationship-based service.
description_long:
First Federal Savings & Loan Association of Greensburg is a locally-owned financial institution headquartered in Greensburg, Indiana. The institution emphasizes its role as a community member committed to understanding and serving local financial needs with personal service and integrity. Founded on principles of building lasting customer relationships, the organization positions itself as an alternative to larger national banks by leveraging local knowledge and decision-making authority.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of banking services spanning personal and business segments. Personal banking includes checking accounts (with multiple tiers: Basic, Free, Shelby Rewards, Angie's Choice, and Prime Rewards), savings accounts, money market accounts, health savings accounts, and personal lending solutions. Business banking features tailored checking accounts and lending products. Additional services include online banking with daily limits up to $9,999.99, bill pay (standard delivery free, rush delivery $14.95), ATM and debit card access with $505 daily withdrawal and $1,000 daily transaction limits, wire transfers (domestic incoming/outgoing at $20 each), safe deposit boxes, notary services, and coin counting for customers.

First Federal differentiates itself through its locally-owned status and emphasis on personalized service and community integration. The institution maintains physical presence at Greensburg Crossing and offers 24-hour phone support (833-838-1348). Its approach contrasts with large national banks by promising relationship-based lending with guidance and flexible solutions. The fee structure is transparent and detailed, with reasonable pricing on most services—free internet banking, free night deposit service, and free notary services for customers.

The institution serves traditional banking needs competently but operates with limitations typical of smaller regional banks. While offering modern conveniences like online banking and bill pay, it lacks mention of advanced digital features, mobile apps, or investment services. The $505 daily ATM withdrawal limit and $1,000 debit card daily transaction limit may constrain users with higher cash needs. As a federally-insured FDIC member, it provides standard deposit protection, but customers should verify whether specific service needs match its community-bank scale.
services: Personal checking accounts (multiple tiers: Basic, Free, Shelby Rewards, Angie's Choice, Prime Rewards)
Savings accounts and money market accounts
Online banking with bill pay functionality
Safe deposit boxes (multiple sizes, rental rates available upon request)
pros: Free internet banking with $9,999.99 daily transaction limit
Free bill pay with standard delivery (rush option available for $14.95)
Free night deposit service for after-hours deposits
Free notary services for customers (non-customers pay $1.00)
Transparent, detailed fee schedule published on website
24-hour phone support via direct teller line (833-838-1348)
FDIC member with standard deposit insurance protection
Locally-owned with emphasis on personal service and community relationships
cons: $505 daily ATM withdrawal limit may be restrictive for high-cash users
$1,000 daily debit card transaction limit restricts larger purchases
No mention of mobile app or advanced digital banking features
Limited geographic footprint as single-location institution may inconvenience customers outside Greensburg area
Various account maintenance fees ($0.75/month for dormant accounts, $5.00+ for service requests) add up for inactive or high-service-need customers
best_for: Local Greensburg, Indiana residents seeking relationship-based personal banking
Small business owners needing tailored local lending decisions and business checking
Customers prioritizing community bank service over digital-first banking experiences
Individuals comfortable with traditional banking channels (phone, in-person, mail)
diagnosis: First Federal Savings & Loan is best suited for Greensburg-area residents and small business owners who value local decision-making, personal relationships, and traditional banking services over cutting-edge digital features. The main caveat is that daily transaction limits ($505 ATM, $1,000 debit) and single-location operations may limit utility for high-volume users or customers outside the immediate service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
